title: New Hope in the Fight Against Pancreatic Cancer
subtitle: Recent medical breakthroughs offer better survival rates, though a total cure remains out of reach.

summary.en: Medical researchers are celebrating a significant development in treating pancreatic cancer. A new drug called daraxonrasib has been shown to double survival times in patients. While this is a major step forward, experts warn that the disease remains extremely difficult to defeat. Pharmaceutical companies are now investing heavily in this area of research. Despite these advances, doctors emphasize that early detection and prevention are still the best tools for saving lives.
summary.zh: 醫學研究人員正慶祝胰臟癌治療上的重大發展。一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥已被證實能讓患者的存活時間翻倍。儘管這是一大進步，專家仍警告該疾病依然極難克服。製藥公司目前正投入大量資金於此領域的研究。儘管有這些進展，醫師們強調，早期偵測與預防仍是拯救生命的最佳工具。

paragraphs.en: The medical world is seeing a wave of progress in the fight against cancer, with new research offering fresh hope for patients facing some of the most difficult forms of the disease. Following recent discussions at a major conference in Chicago, experts are highlighting a significant breakthrough in treating pancreatic cancer. This development comes as pharmaceutical companies increase their investment in new technologies designed to target specific genetic mutations that drive tumor growth.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學界在對抗癌症的戰役中正見證一波進展，新的研究為面臨某些最棘手癌症類型的患者帶來了新希望。繼近期在芝加哥舉行的一場大型會議討論後，專家們強調了胰臟癌治療上的重大突破。這項發展正值製藥公司增加對新技術的投資，這些技術旨在鎖定導致腫瘤生長的特定基因突變。

paragraphs.en: According to Deutsche Welle (DW), a new drug known as daraxonrasib has shown the ability to double the survival time for patients with pancreatic cancer. In a recent study, patients taking this medication lived for a median of 13.2 months, compared to only 6.6 months for those receiving standard chemotherapy. Beyond living longer, patients reported a better quality of life and fewer severe side effects. Dietrich Ruess, a medical director at the Freiburg University Medical Center, described the results as a revolutionary breakthrough for those suffering from this aggressive disease.
paragraphs.zh: 根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）報導，一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥已展現出能讓胰臟癌患者存活時間翻倍的能力。在近期的一項研究中，服用此藥物的患者中位存活期為十三點二個月，相較之下，接受標準化學治療的患者僅有六點六個月。除了延長壽命，患者也回報了較佳的生活品質，且嚴重副作用較少。弗萊堡大學醫學中心（Freiburg University Medical Center）的醫學主任迪特里希·魯斯（Dietrich Ruess）形容，對於飽受這種侵襲性疾病折磨的患者而言，這些結果是一項革命性的突破。

paragraphs.en: This new treatment works by focusing on the RAS gene. This gene is responsible for a protein that controls how cells grow and divide. In about 90% of pancreatic cancer cases, a mutation keeps this gene permanently active, which causes tumors to form and grow rapidly. By specifically targeting this protein, researchers hope to stop the cancer from spreading. Dieter Saur, a professor at the Technical University of Munich, noted that this is one of the most important clinical developments in the field in many years.
paragraphs.zh: 這種新療法的作用機制是鎖定 RAS 基因。該基因負責製造一種控制細胞生長與分裂的蛋白質。在約百分之九十的胰臟癌病例中，基因突變會使該基因處於永久活躍狀態，進而導致腫瘤形成並快速生長。透過精準鎖定這種蛋白質，研究人員希望阻止癌症擴散。慕尼黑工業大學（Technical University of Munich）教授迪特·索爾（Dieter Saur）指出，這是該領域多年來最重要的臨床發展之一。

paragraphs.en: Industry interest in this area is also growing rapidly. As reported by STAT News, the company Johnson & Johnson recently announced it will spend $1 billion to purchase a startup called Firefly Bio. This move is part of a larger effort to expand the company's work with KRAS inhibitors—a type of drug that stops the activity of the KRAS protein. Before recent successes, scientists struggled to create drugs that could successfully latch onto this protein, which they often compared to a slippery, greasy ball. The success of these new treatments is changing how the industry views these previously untreatable tumors.
paragraphs.zh: 產業界對此領域的興趣也正快速增長。根據 STAT 新聞報導，嬌生公司（Johnson & Johnson）近期宣布將斥資十億美元收購一家名為 Firefly Bio 的新創公司。此舉是該公司擴大研發 KRAS 抑制劑計畫的一部分，這類藥物能阻斷 KRAS 蛋白質的活性。在近期取得成功之前，科學家一直難以開發出能成功附著於這種蛋白質的藥物，他們常將其比喻為一顆濕滑且油膩的球。這些新療法的成功，正在改變產業界對這些過去被視為無法治療的腫瘤之看法。

paragraphs.en: Despite the excitement, health experts are urging caution. Susanne Weg-Remers, a physician at the German Cancer Research Center, warned that calling the drug a cure is incorrect. In most cases, a diagnosis of pancreatic cancer remains a very serious situation with a low survival rate. Only about 11% of patients are still alive five years after their initial diagnosis. Because the pancreas is located deep inside the body, the disease often develops without any clear warning signs, making it very hard to detect until it is advanced.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管令人振奮，健康專家仍呼籲保持審慎。德國癌症研究中心（German Cancer Research Center）的醫師蘇珊·韋格-雷默斯（Susanne Weg-Remers）警告，將此藥物稱為「治癒」是不正確的。在大多數情況下，胰臟癌的診斷仍是非常嚴重的狀況，且存活率偏低。僅約百分之十一的患者在初次診斷後能存活超過五年。由於胰臟位於人體深處，該疾病往往在沒有任何明顯警訊的情況下發展，導致在病情惡化前極難被偵測到。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the global fight against cancer has seen significant progress over the last few decades. Survival chances have improved for many types of the disease, and mortality rates in the UK have dropped by nearly a quarter since the 1970s. The World Health Organization reports that more than 50 million people are currently alive after receiving a cancer diagnosis in the last five years. However, the availability of high-quality care remains unevenly distributed across the world, and the burden on healthcare systems continues to grow.
paragraphs.zh: 根據衛報（The Guardian）報導，全球對抗癌症的戰役在過去幾十年中已取得顯著進展。許多癌症類型的存活機率皆有所提升，英國的死亡率自一九七○年代以來已下降了近四分之一。世界衛生組織（World Health Organization）報告指出，目前有超過五千萬人在確診癌症後的五年內依然存活。然而，高品質醫療資源的取得在全球各地仍分布不均，且醫療體系所承受的負擔持續增加。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, researchers are exploring various ways to improve patient outcomes. While surgery remains the best option for those who are diagnosed early, it is rarely possible due to the late discovery of the disease. Scientists are now investigating other methods, including mRNA vaccines, which are designed to prevent the cancer from returning after initial treatment. These vaccines work by teaching the immune system to recognize and destroy cancer cells more effectively.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，研究人員正探索各種改善患者預後的方法。雖然手術仍是早期確診患者的最佳選擇，但由於疾病發現較晚，這種機會往往微乎其微。科學家目前正研究其他方法，包括 mRNA 疫苗，旨在預防癌症在初步治療後復發。這些疫苗的作用原理是教導免疫系統更有效地辨識並摧毀癌細胞。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, while the development of drugs like daraxonrasib represents a major step forward, the medical community agrees that there is still much work to be done. As experts continue to share their findings at international summits, the focus remains on turning these incremental scientific discoveries into accessible, life-saving treatments for patients everywhere. For now, the combination of better drugs, improved testing, and a deeper understanding of genetics provides a more hopeful future than ever before.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，雖然像 daraxonrasib 這類藥物的開發代表了一大進步，但醫學界一致認為仍有許多工作待完成。隨著專家持續在國際高峰會上分享研究發現，重點仍在於將這些漸進式的科學發現轉化為各地患者皆能取得、且能挽救生命的療法。目前而言，更好的藥物、改良的檢測技術，以及對遺傳學更深入的理解，共同提供了一個比以往任何時候都更具希望的未來。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To cause or force something to happen or develop.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「駕駛」，這裡指促使腫瘤生長。文中：new technologies designed to target specific genetic mutations that drive tumor growth.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To attach or connect firmly to something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡形容藥物分子與蛋白質結合的過程。文中：scientists struggled to create drugs that could successfully latch onto this protein, which they often compared to a slippery, greasy ball.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A heavy load or responsibility that is difficult to manage.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指醫療系統承受的壓力。文中：the burden on healthcare systems continues to grow.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Monday briefing: Are we any closer to a cure for cancer?

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: STAT+: J&J spends $1 billion to enter KRAS race for cancer treatments

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Pancreatic cancer: New drug extends lives but isn't a cure

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope in Cancer Fight
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. These new drugs work by helping the immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden. One specific pill has shown the ability to double survival times for patients with pancreatic cancer. Another treatment helps patients with various common cancers by removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that tumors use to hide. While these results are early, experts believe they represent a significant step forward in oncology.

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope for Cancer Patients
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. Scientists have developed new drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for some patients. Additionally, hospitals are adopting a new, non-invasive urine test for bladder cancer. These developments represent a shift toward more manageable and less painful care.

quiz.question: According to the study mentioned, how does the new drug daraxonrasib compare to standard chemotherapy for pancreatic cancer patients?

options.A: It cures the disease in 90% of patients.
options.B: It doubles the median survival time of patients.
options.C: It reduces the need for surgical intervention.
options.D: It is significantly cheaper than standard treatments.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，研究顯示服用 daraxonrasib 的患者中位生存期為 13.2 個月，而接受標準化療的患者僅為 6.6 個月，正好是兩倍。選項 A 錯誤，因為 90% 是指 RAS 基因突變的比例，而非治癒率。選項 C 文章未提及藥物與手術需求的直接關聯。選項 D 文章中並未討論藥物的成本問題。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the difficulty scientists previously faced in treating tumors related to the KRAS protein?

options.A: The protein was too small to be detected by modern medical equipment.
options.B: The protein was resistant to all known forms of pharmaceutical research.
options.C: The protein's physical structure made it hard for drugs to attach to it.
options.D: The protein was only found in patients with late-stage cancer.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段提到科學家曾將 KRAS 蛋白比喻為「滑溜且油膩的球」（a slippery, greasy ball），這暗示了該蛋白的表面特性使得藥物難以附著（latch onto）。選項 A 錯誤，文中未提到大小問題。選項 B 過於誇大，文中僅提到難以開發藥物，而非對所有研究產生抗性。選項 D 文中並未說明該蛋白僅存在於末期癌症。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'turning these incremental scientific discoveries into accessible, life-saving treatments,' what does the word 'incremental' mean?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。Incremental 的字面意思是「增量的、漸進的」，在文中用來描述科學發現是一步步積累的過程，而非一次性的巨大飛躍。選項 B、C、D 皆與該詞的語義不符。在醫學研究情境中，科學進步通常是透過長期的微小發現累積而成，因此「漸進的」最符合上下文語境。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the fight against cancer?

options.A: Pancreatic cancer is now considered a fully curable disease due to new vaccines.
options.B: Pharmaceutical companies are the only entities responsible for medical breakthroughs.
options.C: Global healthcare systems have successfully eliminated the gap in cancer care.
options.D: Progress in genetics and new drug development offers hope, but significant challenges remain.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。本文整體架構先介紹了針對胰臟癌的新藥突破與基因研究進展，隨後在第五段與最後一段提醒讀者，胰臟癌仍難以診斷且治癒率低，全球醫療資源分配亦不均，強調了科學進步雖帶來希望，但仍有許多工作待完成。選項 A 過於樂觀，文章明確指出並非治癒。選項 B 忽略了研究機構與醫生的貢獻。選項 C 與文章第六段提到的醫療資源分配不均矛盾。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
